title: crotalaria cupricola
intro: Crotalaria cupricola, also known as the copper weed or golden bush, is a plant species that belongs to the legume family and is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Crotalaria cupricola include its ability to reduce fever, treat skin infections, and aid in the management of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Crotalaria cupricola can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal uses, and it is also used as a component in traditional folk remedies.
